note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to establish a three-dimensional coculture module of epithelial cells using nanofibrous membranes. The goal is to create a model for studying cell interactions and responses under specific conditions.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A scientist in a lab coat introduces the experiment, setting up a controlled environment with humidity control and temperature monitoring.",
    "The scientist prepares nanofibrous membranes by placing them on slides and allowing them to air dry before heating them to 100°C.",
    "Cells are cultured in a humidified chamber, and the scientist uses a microscope to capture scanning electron microscope images.",
    "The scientist then transfers cells from one container to another using a pipette, ensuring precision and accuracy."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Nanofibrous membranes, epithelial cells (NIH3T3 and MLE-12 lung epithelial), culture media, incubators, microscopes, pipettes"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Humidity control system, temperature monitor, incubator, microscope, pipette, nanofibrous membrane preparation setup"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Humidity levels adjusted between 40-50% at the beginning and 25-30% at the end of the experiment.",
    "Temperature set to 100°C for membrane heating.",
    "Cell cultures maintained in a humidified chamber.",
    "Microscope settings adjusted for capturing high-resolution images."
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Transition from setting up the experimental environment to preparing and culturing the cells.",
    "Transition from adjusting humidity and temperature to transferring cells using a pipette."
  ],
